name: Polyester Resin Composites Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Polyester Resin Composites. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for polyester resin composite cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength optimal for polyester resin composite processing

spotSize.description: Optimal beam spot diameter for precision cleaning

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning process

pulseWidth.description: Optimal pulse duration for efficient material removal with minimal heat affected zone

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for efficient coverage and material removal

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning

fluence.description: Energy density threshold for effective Polyester Resin Composites cleaning - Energy density threshold for composite material cleaning considering matrix and reinforcement properties

power.description: Typical range for industrial 1064 nm laser cleaning of polymer composites to balance efficiency and heat input; starting at 100 W avoids thermal damage to polyester's low melting point (~260°C). Max safe ~300 W at high speeds to prevent substrate ablation. Source: Laserax manufacturer specs and studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on composite surface prep.

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contamination removal; slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy contaminants like oils, faster (2000-5000 mm/s) for light dust. Recommended 2000 mm/s for optimal cleaning without overheating polyester's low thermal conductivity (~0.2 W/m·K). Source: IPG Photonics equipment manuals and Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) case studies on polymer composites.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime (5-100 ns) preferred over ps/fs for cost-effective 1064 nm cleaning of composites; 20 ns minimizes heat-affected zone (HAZ <50 μm) by reducing thermal diffusion in polyester. Ps/fs (e.g., <1 ps) for ultra-precise but less common industrially. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on polymer ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical 10-100 kHz for pulsed cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for polyester composites to achieve fast coverage while limiting heat accumulation (trade-off: higher rates speed up process but risk melting if >50 kHz without high speed). Source: Academic literature in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2021) on fiber-reinforced polymers.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe ablation of contaminants; damage threshold for polyester composites ~3-5 J/cm² due to reflectivity (~5-10% at 1064 nm) and low thermal conductivity. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² for effective cleaning without substrate damage. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Composites Part B: Engineering (2017) and SPIE proceedings on laser cleaning.

spot_size.description: Typical beam diameter for industrial cleaning; 100-200 μm for fine surface prep on composites, 300-500 μm for large areas. 200 μm recommended for polyester to ensure uniform energy distribution and avoid localized overheating. Source: Coherent Inc. equipment specifications and industry standards from Laser Institute of America.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy layers; 2 passes recommended for complete removal on polyester composites without excessive heat buildup. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Journal of Materials Processing Technology (2022) on resin composite cleaning.

overlap_ratio.description: 30-80% ensures uniform coverage; 50% recommended for polyester to balance efficiency and avoid uneven heating or missed spots during scan. Higher overlap (70-80%) for heavy contamination. Source: Process optimization studies in Optics & Laser Technology (2019) on polymer surface treatment.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser cleaning of fiber-reinforced polyester composites: Ablation thresholds and surface morphology
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Reverdy, F. et al., Optics and Lasers in Engineering, Vol. 71, 2015, DOI: 10.1016/j.optlaseng.2015.03.012
laserDamageThreshold.context: Glass fiber-reinforced unsaturated polyester resin composite (50 wt% glass fibers), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 10 ns pulse duration, room temperature (25°C), atmospheric pressure

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation of Fiber-Reinforced Polymer Composites: A Review
ablationThreshold.citation: Punshon, C. S., et al., Journal of Laser Applications, 2018, DOI: 10.2351/1.5026123
ablationThreshold.context: Glass fiber reinforced polyester resin composite (50 wt% glass fiber), room temperature (25°C), measured using 532 nm Nd:YAG laser with 10 ns pulse duration
